conversations.say: Inez, management flagged the unpreserved camera interval from the audit notice. We need to talk about our disclosure boundary before we answer anyone else.
conversations.offer: To coordinate our account of the building service route and protect the tenant.
conversations.request: Your agreement on how we explain the camera gap without exposing anyone.

conversations.say: Rafi, let's compare our shift change records and collectors for the building service routes so we keep our shift details straight.
conversations.offer: Exact building shift times and routine service logs.
conversations.request: Your records of transactions and shift changes.

conversations.say: Morgan, before I share any records about the audit notice or access gaps, what do fair treatment and a bounded account mean in your report?
conversations.offer: Building rhythms and access practice context.
conversations.request: A guarantee of precise attribution, resident anonymity, and defensible handling of the audit.

public_statement: The service routes in our building are practical passages for working people, not a criminal conspiracy. An audit flag does not mean a murder.

commitments.promise: Coordinate our disclosure boundary regarding the camera gap before speaking publicly.

goal_progress: Approached Inez to discuss the camera interval audit before answering Morgan.
Initiated comparison of shift-change details with Rafi.
Asked Morgan what fair treatment and bounded account mean in the final report.
reasoning_summary: Dee Nowak approaches Inez Baptiste first to discuss the unpreserved camera interval audit notice (V2F026) and align on a protective disclosure boundary. Dee also compares shift change details with Rafi Calderón and asks Morgan Shaw for explicit terms of fair treatment and a bounded account before offering building records.
